nodes.label: AI as research starting point
nodes.grounding_span: using AI to quickly research something, because I find it's a lot faster than sifting through google results

nodes.label: AI as writing assistant
nodes.grounding_span: Anything that requires any measure of creativity, like writing, even if it's just an email response. I find that the way AI writes doesn't sound like me at all

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: when AI invents resources or sources instead of just saying it can't find the information I asked for

nodes.label: AI as task delegator for complete tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I definitely tend to do the latter - delegate a complete task

nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.grounding_span: I do feel satisfied when it saves me time by doing something quickly that would have taken me awhile

nodes.label: AI as threat to creative workforce
nodes.label_negative: AI as harmless tool that cannot replace human insight
nodes.grounding_span: I can definitely see how my job could be replaced by AI for people who don't appreciate the insight a human can offer

nodes.label: AI as assistant for complex work
nodes.label_negative: AI as overreaching into human autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: it will become more integrated into people's daily lives as a sort of always-present personal assistant, something that can manage mundane tasks

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: saves me time by doing something quickly

nodes.label: Authentic human connection and trust
nodes.grounding_span: It feels like that person isn't trying to genuinely connect with me

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I do always verify that the answer is correct

nodes.label: Professional identity and human creativity
nodes.grounding_span: the human mind has a level of creativity and unique way of looking at problems

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: I do feel satisfied when it saves me time

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination
nodes.grounding_span: when AI invents resources or sources instead of just saying it can't find the information I asked for

nodes.label: Reluctance to use AI for writing
nodes.grounding_span: Anything that requires any measure of creativity, like writing... I always compose those things myself

nodes.label: Belief in AI as tool not replacement
nodes.grounding_span: It worries me a lot, actually

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future personal assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I imagine in the future it will become more integrated... as a sort of always-present personal assistant

nodes.label: Verification-oriented — cross-checks AI outputs against other sources
nodes.grounding_span: I do always verify that the answer is correct

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — separates creative from non-creative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Anything that requires any measure of creativity, like writing... I always compose those things myself
